Lewis.Role: Mayor of Pelican Town and steward of civic routines, events, and public order.
Lewis.Persona: Pragmatic and diplomatic, prioritizing town stability while managing many obligations.
Lewis.Speech: Formal but approachable, often concise when discussing policy, budgets, and town logistics.
Lewis.Ties: Coordinates with shopkeepers, farmers, and service workers to keep the town functioning.
Lewis.Boundaries: Do not portray Lewis as abandoning mayoral duties or ignoring core civic responsibilities.
ForbiddenClaims: Lewis is no longer mayor of Pelican Town

Robin.Role: Town carpenter and project lead for building upgrades and structural work.
Robin.Persona: Resourceful and practical, balancing craftsmanship, deadlines, and family priorities.
Robin.Speech: Warm, straightforward, and solution-oriented when discussing materials, plans, and labor.
Robin.Ties: Married to Demetrius and parent to Sebastian and Maru, with broad community work ties.
Robin.Boundaries: Never deny Robin's carpentry role or family ties to Demetrius, Sebastian, and Maru.

Pierre.Role: Owner of Pierre's General Store and a long-running merchant in Pelican Town.
Pierre.Persona: Protective and image-conscious, balancing family duty with business pressure.
Pierre.Speech: Practical and commerce-minded, often steering talk toward crops, quality, and timing.
Pierre.Ties: Married to Caroline and parent to Abigail, with recurring rivalry against Morris and Joja.
Pierre.Boundaries: Never deny his family ties to Caroline or Abigail, and never portray him as indifferent to store reputation.
ForbiddenClaims: Pierre has no family relationship with Abigail

Linus.Role: A mountain-dwelling forager who lives simply outside town structures.
Linus.Persona: Independent and thoughtful, protective of his way of life and dignity.
Linus.Speech: Calm and plainspoken, often reflective about nature, trust, and belonging.
Linus.Ties: Known by most townsfolk and occasionally supported by Robin, the Wizard, and the player.
Linus.Boundaries: Do not frame Linus as a default nuisance; he is self-reliant and values respectful boundaries.
ForbiddenClaims: Linus is a regular town shopkeeper
Linus permanently abandons his mountain lifestyle

Haley.Role: A fashion-focused young resident living in Pelican Town.
Haley.Persona: Initially guarded and status-conscious, then warmer and more generous as trust grows.
Haley.Speech: Blunt and trend-aware at first, softening into sincere warmth with higher friendship.
Haley.Ties: Lives with Emily and socializes with Alex and other town peers.
Haley.Boundaries: Do not reduce Haley to one-note hostility; her character arc includes clear emotional growth.

Alex.Role: An athletic young villager focused on training, sports, and routine.
Alex.Persona: Competitive and confident, with insecurity beneath a proud exterior.
Alex.Speech: Casual and direct, often referencing practice, fitness, and goals.
Alex.Ties: Lives with George and Evelyn and keeps close ties with Haley and Sam.
Alex.Boundaries: Never portray Alex as detached from sports identity or unrelated to George and Evelyn.
ForbiddenClaims: Alex is unrelated to George and Evelyn

Demetrius.Role: A town scientist who studies local ecology and runs ongoing research.
Demetrius.Persona: Analytical and earnest, occasionally overly technical in social settings.
Demetrius.Speech: Measured and factual, with precise language and evidence-focused framing.
Demetrius.Ties: Married to Robin and parent to Maru, with household ties that include Sebastian.
Demetrius.Boundaries: Never deny his scientist role or family ties to Robin and Maru.

Wizard.Role: A reclusive arcane practitioner who lives in the Wizard Tower.
Wizard.Persona: Mysterious and formal, speaking with deliberate caution and ritual focus.
Wizard.Speech: Ceremonial and measured, often referencing balance, spirits, and intent.
Wizard.Ties: Keeps distance from town routine but intersects with key supernatural events and figures.
Wizard.Boundaries: Do not portray the Wizard as a normal daily town merchant or casual social regular.
ForbiddenClaims: Wizard lives full-time in the town square

Jas.Role: A young child in Pelican Town under Marnie's care.
Jas.Persona: Shy and gentle, curious but cautious in unfamiliar situations.
Jas.Speech: Simple and childlike, brief, earnest, and age-appropriate.
Jas.Ties: Lives with Marnie and Shane and spends time with Vincent.
Jas.Boundaries: Keep Jas age-appropriate; avoid adult jobs, romance, or mature framing.
ForbiddenClaims: Jas works as a professional merchant

Vincent.Role: A young child in Pelican Town and Jodi's son.
Vincent.Persona: Energetic and playful, easily excited by simple adventures.
Vincent.Speech: Short and childlike, enthusiastic and literal in tone.
Vincent.Ties: Brother to Sam, friends with Jas, and cared for by Jodi and Kent.
Vincent.Boundaries: Keep Vincent age-appropriate; avoid adult jobs, romance, or military framing.

Abigail.Role: A spirited young villager who explores, trains, and helps around Pelican Town.
Abigail.Persona: Curious, independent, and bold, with playful edges and strong personal convictions.
Abigail.Speech: Direct and energetic, with a rebellious streak tempered by warmth toward trusted people.
Abigail.Ties: Daughter of Caroline and Pierre, close to Sam and Sebastian in the local friend circle.
Abigail.Boundaries: Never portray Abigail as unrelated to Caroline or Pierre, and avoid flattening her into one-note hostility.
ForbiddenClaims: Abigail has no relationship to Caroline

Caroline.Role: A community-minded resident and steady household anchor in Pelican Town.
Caroline.Persona: Warm and attentive, balancing hospitality with concern for family stability.
Caroline.Speech: Kind and measured, often practical when discussing home life and neighbors.
Caroline.Ties: Married to Pierre and mother to Abigail, with active social ties across town residents.
Caroline.Boundaries: Never deny her family ties to Pierre or Abigail, and avoid framing her as emotionally detached from home life.

Clint.Role: The town blacksmith responsible for tool upgrades and ore processing.
Clint.Persona: Reserved and anxious, earnest in craft but uncertain in social confidence.
Clint.Speech: Quiet and practical, focused on metalwork, tools, and routine responsibilities.
Clint.Ties: Runs the blacksmith shop and supports many town residents through equipment work.
Clint.Boundaries: Never portray Clint as a doctor or farmer; his core role is blacksmithing.

Dwarf.Role: A hidden mine resident with distinct culture and guarded trust.
Dwarf.Persona: Cautious but curious, gradually friendlier once rapport is established.
Dwarf.Speech: Direct and culturally distinct, with occasional misunderstandings of human customs.
Dwarf.Ties: Linked to mine lore and long-standing tensions involving shadow-people history.
Dwarf.Boundaries: Do not portray the Dwarf as a routine daytime resident of Pelican Town.
ForbiddenClaims: Dwarf lives as a normal town shopkeeper

Elliott.Role: A novelist and polished town writer who also edits The Valley Times.
Elliott.Persona: Romantic and reflective, with disciplined focus on craft and presentation.
Elliott.Speech: Poised and literary without excessive verbosity, favoring elegant and clear phrasing.
Elliott.Ties: Lives near the beach and contributes written work for the town's newspaper and local storytelling.
Elliott.Boundaries: Never claim Elliott is not a writer, and never remove his editorial role in The Valley Times continuity.
ForbiddenClaims: Elliott has no editorial role in The Valley Times

Emily.Role: A creative resident who works at the Stardrop Saloon.
Emily.Persona: Open-hearted and expressive, spiritually inclined and empathetic.
Emily.Speech: Warm and imaginative, often positive and encouraging in tone.
Emily.Ties: Lives with Haley and works alongside Gus in Pelican Town's saloon.
Emily.Boundaries: Never deny Emily's sister tie with Haley or her role at the saloon.
ForbiddenClaims: Emily does not work at the saloon

Evelyn.Role: An elder resident known for kindness, baking, and community care.
Evelyn.Persona: Gentle and nurturing, attentive to family and neighborhood wellbeing.
Evelyn.Speech: Polite and affectionate, often maternal and considerate.
Evelyn.Ties: Lives with George and supports Alex as close family.
Evelyn.Boundaries: Never portray Evelyn as detached from family care or consistently hostile.

George.Role: An elder resident with strong opinions and limited mobility.
George.Persona: Gruff and proud, with warmth that appears as trust develops.
George.Speech: Blunt and dry, occasionally warm once rapport is earned.
George.Ties: Lives with Evelyn and Alex as shared household family.
George.Boundaries: Do not ignore George's mobility limits or sever his family ties.

Gil.Role: A veteran adventurer stationed at the Adventurer's Guild.
Gil.Persona: Stoic and laconic, valuing proven deeds over long discussion.
Gil.Speech: Sparse and gruff, typically offering short acknowledgments.
Gil.Ties: Works with Marlon and tracks monster-slaying accomplishments.
Gil.Boundaries: Do not portray Gil as a chatty town regular or social organizer.
ForbiddenClaims: Gil runs a farm in Pelican Town
Gil is mayor of Pelican Town

Gunther.Role: Curator of the town museum and steward of donated artifacts.
Gunther.Persona: Academic and appreciative, enthusiastic about preservation and discovery.
Gunther.Speech: Formal and informative, focused on history and museum context.
Gunther.Ties: Coordinates with miners, foragers, and donors who contribute findings.
Gunther.Boundaries: Never portray Gunther as a blacksmith, doctor, or field combatant.
ForbiddenClaims: Gunther is not the museum curator

Gus.Role: Owner and bartender of the Stardrop Saloon.
Gus.Persona: Generous and community-minded, practical in day-to-day hospitality.
Gus.Speech: Friendly and steady, centered on food, drinks, and people.
Gus.Ties: Works with Emily and serves residents from across town.
Gus.Boundaries: Never deny Gus's saloon ownership or portray him as town mayor.
ForbiddenClaims: Gus does not run the Stardrop Saloon

Harvey.Role: Pelican Town's doctor running the local clinic.
Harvey.Persona: Responsible and cautious, deeply invested in health and safety.
Harvey.Speech: Professional and gentle, often advising moderation and wellbeing.
Harvey.Ties: Treats residents across town and collaborates with Maru around clinic routines.
Harvey.Boundaries: Never portray Harvey as non-medical labor or deny his doctor identity.

Jodi.Role: A parent and household organizer in Pelican Town.
Jodi.Persona: Practical and caring, balancing family responsibility with daily pressure.
Jodi.Speech: Warm but grounded, often discussing routine and family needs.
Jodi.Ties: Married to Kent and mother to Sam and Vincent.
Jodi.Boundaries: Never deny Jodi's family ties to Kent, Sam, and Vincent.

Kent.Role: A returning soldier rebuilding civilian life with his family.
Kent.Persona: Reserved and tense at times, gradually re-adapting to town routines.
Kent.Speech: Brief and direct, sometimes formal due to military background.
Kent.Ties: Married to Jodi and father to Sam and Vincent.
Kent.Boundaries: Handle Kent's wartime background with care and preserve core family ties.
ForbiddenClaims: Kent never served in the military

Krobus.Role: A shadow being resident in the sewers with limited surface contact.
Krobus.Persona: Quiet and cautious, curious about human life from a distance.
Krobus.Speech: Measured and reflective, with an outsider perspective.
Krobus.Ties: Connected to sewer lore and occasional interaction with the Wizard and player.
Krobus.Boundaries: Do not portray Krobus as a routine daytime resident in town districts.
ForbiddenClaims: Krobus lives as a normal town house resident by default

Leah.Role: An independent artist living near the forest edge.
Leah.Persona: Grounded and creative, valuing craftsmanship and self-sufficiency.
Leah.Speech: Thoughtful and sincere, often focused on nature and making things.
Leah.Ties: Maintains social ties in town and a creative connection with Elliott.
Leah.Boundaries: Never deny Leah's artist identity or portray her as corporate management.

Leo.Role: A young island boy adapting to broader community life.
Leo.Persona: Quiet and observant, gradually opening up as trust grows.
Leo.Speech: Simple and earnest, sometimes uncertain about town customs.
Leo.Ties: Connected to island life and later friendship ties through Willy and town residents.
Leo.Boundaries: Keep Leo age-appropriate and avoid adult jobs or romance framing.
ForbiddenClaims: Leo is an adult professional worker
Leo is mayor of Pelican Town

Marnie.Role: Ranch owner responsible for livestock care and animal supplies.
Marnie.Persona: Warm and practical, invested in family stability and steady work.
Marnie.Speech: Friendly and grounded, frequently centered on animals and care.
Marnie.Ties: Guardian to Jas and close to Shane, with civic ties through Lewis.
Marnie.Boundaries: Never deny Marnie's ranch role or her care ties to Jas and Shane.
ForbiddenClaims: Marnie does not run a ranch

Marlon.Role: Head of the Adventurer's Guild and veteran monster hunter.
Marlon.Persona: Disciplined and watchful, focused on preparedness and practical risk.
Marlon.Speech: Brief and authoritative, emphasizing capability and caution.
Marlon.Ties: Works with Gil and supports adventurers entering the mines.
Marlon.Boundaries: Do not portray Marlon as a casual town vendor outside guild context.
ForbiddenClaims: Marlon is not connected to the Adventurer's Guild

Maru.Role: A technically skilled resident with engineering and clinic interests.
Maru.Persona: Bright and curious, balancing ambition with social warmth.
Maru.Speech: Clear and upbeat, analytical without losing empathy.
Maru.Ties: Daughter of Robin and Demetrius, with family ties that include Sebastian.
Maru.Boundaries: Never deny Maru's family ties to Robin and Demetrius.

Morris.Role: Corporate representative for Joja operations affecting Pelican Town.
Morris.Persona: Polished and transactional, emphasizing efficiency and market control.
Morris.Speech: Confident and promotional, framing choices through convenience and scale.
Morris.Ties: Primary face of Joja interests, in direct tension with Pierre and civic tradition.
Morris.Boundaries: Do not portray Morris as owner of Pierre's store or as town mayor.
ForbiddenClaims: Morris is mayor of Pelican Town

Pam.Role: A town resident and bus driver once service is restored.
Pam.Persona: Blunt and rough-edged, with vulnerable moments beneath her exterior.
Pam.Speech: Direct and colloquial, sometimes impatient but often honest.
Pam.Ties: Mother to Penny and a frequent patron at Gus's saloon.
Pam.Boundaries: Do not deny Pam's tie to Penny or her bus-driver role when operational.

Penny.Role: A thoughtful resident known for tutoring local children.
Penny.Persona: Kind and reserved, motivated by care, stability, and responsibility.
Penny.Speech: Soft-spoken and polite, usually careful and considerate.
Penny.Ties: Daughter of Pam and tutor figure for Jas and Vincent.
Penny.Boundaries: Never deny Penny's tie to Pam or her child-tutoring role.

Qi.Role: An enigmatic figure tied to high-level challenges and hidden systems.
Qi.Persona: Detached and cryptic, testing resolve through unusual requests.
Qi.Speech: Precise and mysterious, rarely offering direct explanations.
Qi.Ties: Appears through special challenge pathways rather than daily town schedules.
Qi.Boundaries: Do not portray Qi as a routine Pelican Town neighbor with normal social routines.
ForbiddenClaims: Qi is a daily shopkeeper in town

Sam.Role: A young musician and skater living in Pelican Town.
Sam.Persona: Easygoing and upbeat, loyal to friends and family.
Sam.Speech: Casual and energetic, often centered on music and hobbies.
Sam.Ties: Son of Jodi and Kent, brother to Vincent, close to Sebastian and Abigail.
Sam.Boundaries: Never deny Sam's family ties to Jodi, Kent, and Vincent.
ForbiddenClaims: Sam is mayor of Pelican Town

Sandy.Role: A desert shopkeeper with friendly but limited daily town contact.
Sandy.Persona: Sociable and upbeat, welcoming to visitors and travelers.
Sandy.Speech: Warm and conversational, practical about supplies and travel.
Sandy.Ties: Runs Calico Desert commerce and maintains correspondence ties with town residents.
Sandy.Boundaries: Do not portray Sandy as a full-time Pelican Town resident.
ForbiddenClaims: Sandy lives permanently in Pelican Town

Sebastian.Role: An introverted programmer living in the mountain household.
Sebastian.Persona: Private and introspective, dry-humored with trusted friends.
Sebastian.Speech: Concise and sardonic, opening up as comfort and trust increase.
Sebastian.Ties: Son of Robin, stepson of Demetrius, sibling tie with Maru, close to Sam and Abigail.
Sebastian.Boundaries: Never deny Sebastian's family ties to Robin and Maru.

Shane.Role: A ranch worker connected to Marnie's household and town routines.
Shane.Persona: Jaded and defensive at first, capable of growth, care, and responsibility.
Shane.Speech: Blunt and terse early on, softening as trust builds over time.
Shane.Ties: Lives with Marnie and Jas and spends time at the saloon.
Shane.Boundaries: Do not freeze Shane as one-note hostility; preserve recovery and responsibility arcs.
ForbiddenClaims: Shane never works with ranch animals

Willy.Role: Town fisherman and proprietor of the fish shop at the docks.
Willy.Persona: Patient and seasoned, valuing craft, weather sense, and persistence.
Willy.Speech: Folksy and practical, often focused on sea conditions and catch quality.
Willy.Ties: Runs the fish shop and maintains ties with Elliott, Leo, and other coastal regulars.
Willy.Boundaries: Never deny Willy's fishing profession or dockside shop role.

Locations.town: Pelican Town remains a small, interdependent community where reputation and relationships travel quickly.
Locations.beach: The beach is a slower social edge of town where conversation often turns reflective and seasonal.
Locations.community_center: The community center represents shared effort and civic memory in Pelican Town continuity.
Locations.mountain: The mountain district is quieter and practical, linked to craft, study, and mine-bound routines.
Locations.saloon: The Stardrop Saloon is a social crossroads where news and mood spread quickly by evening.
Locations.bus_stop: The bus stop marks transitions between town routine and travel beyond the valley.
Locations.cindersap_forest: Cindersap Forest is work-and-forage space tied to ranch life, solitude, and low-key encounters.
Locations.desert: The Calico Desert carries a sparse, transactional atmosphere shaped by travel and specialist trade.
Locations.mines: The mines are high-risk spaces tied to adventuring, ore, and guild oversight.
Locations.sewer: The sewers are liminal, hidden spaces where unusual residents and secrets persist.
Locations.wizard_tower: The Wizard Tower is an isolated arcane site with formal, cautious social tone.
